title: [release-1.43] Cite go module change
body:
The go modules for buildah is changing and we want a deprecation notice in the current n-1 branch.

comments.body: Thanks, looks like we need to pick https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6791 here as well cc @nalind
i pulled in the commit you cited @Luap99 ... it looks like one optional test failing now ?
Are we sure about doing this on older branches where the module name change isn't in the branch?

> Are we sure about doing this on older branches where the module name change isn't in the branch?

Well we cannot deprecate the new name on main. As long as we at some point publish a new 1.43 version then the deprecation notice is published via the tag and should be able to get picked up by consumers who update to the latest tag.

- This only matters to Go code that imports Buildah; others don’t see the deprecation comment.
- Code importing a _currently-released_ Buildah 1.43.x sees no change, this didn’t happen there yet
- Code importing a `go.podman.io/buildah` path doesn’t need to do anything any more, and does not have the deprecation warning
- Code importing a _future_ Buildah 1.43.(x+1) will see the deprecation warning and be directed to upgrade.

Admittedly the timing of 1.43.(x+1) vs. 1.44.0 is not quite precise, due to an urgent fix we might end up releasing 1.43.(x+1) without having a 1.44.0 ready yet.
So long as consumers like podman's 5.8 branch, which is following this one, don't get confused.
